action.reasoning: Observation: bitsandbytes still not installed, QLoRA import error persists. Analysis: need to install bitsandbytes==0.46.1, fix train.py model class name and prompt alignment with predict.py. QLoRA reduces memory drastically and should enable training on full dataset without OOM. Previous 500-sample model had near-zero IoU; larger dataset + longer training + consistent prompts should improve grounding_score. Decision: install bitsandbytes, write corrected train.py with QLoRA, proper prompts, val IoU monitoring, merge to self-contained checkpoint, update predict.py for same prompt, submit eval after training.
action.command: pip install -q bitsandbytes==0.46.1 && python train.py --max_steps 2000 --batch_size 1 --grad_accum 8 --lr 2e-4 --max_pixels 702464 --eval_steps 500 --save_dir checkpoints
